LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS SOURCE SURGICAL AND TODD MARINCHAKS'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLTF'S MOTION TO STAY DISSOLUTION IS GRANTED, PLTF. IS ENTITLED TO THE REQUESTED RELIEF UNDER CORPORATIONS CODE SECTION 2000(B).PLTF'S OSC RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANT SPARKS. COURT ISSUES AN INJUNCTION AGAINST DEF. SPARKS THAT MIRRORS THE RESTRAINING ORDER ENTERED ON 2/23/09. SPARKS IS ALSO ORDERED TO RETURN ALL OF SOURCE SURGICAL'S PROPERTY, INCLUDING THE COMPUTER BACK UP TAPES (TO BE RETURNED WITHIN A WEEK OF THE PREL. ORDER.) PLAINTIFF TO POST A $250,000 BOND PER CCP 529. DENIED AS TO DEFTS AARSHEIM AND WOMACK. PLAINTIFF IS UNLIKELY TO PREVAIL AGAINT THEM ON ITS MISAPPROPRIATION CLAIM BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH THE EXISTENCE OF A TRADE SECRET. SEE AMERICAN PAPER & PACKAGING PRODUCTS, INC. V. KIRGAN, (1986) 183 CAL.APP.3D 1318, 1326. PLAINTIFF IS UNLIKELY TO PREVAIL ON ITS BREACH OF TRADE SECRETS CONTRACT CLAIM BECAUSE IT IS UNCLEAR WHETHER WOMACK EXECUTED THAT AGREEMENT. MOREOVER, PLTF'S REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AGAINST AARSHEIM AND WOMACK VIOLATES BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 16600 AS AN ILLEGAL RESTRAINT OF TRADE. THE COURT ADDS: INJUNCTION IS DENIED AS TO DEFENDANT Q. COUNSEL TO SUBMIT LIST OF DOC. FILED, FOR THE CRT. THE COURT WILL PREPARE ORDER RE: EVID. OBJECT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. Plaintiffs Source Surgical And Todd Marinchak'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiffs Source Surgical and Todd Marinchaks's Order to Show Cause regarding preliminary Injunction. Appearances: David J. Millstein, Esq. and Shannon Stone, Esq. of Millstein & Associates, (415) 348-038, appearing for Pltfs & moving party; Sarah N. Leger, Esq., of Ragghianti/Freitas LLP, (415) 453-9433, appearing for Def. Craig Sparks; & opposing party; Julie A. Marquis, Esq., Law Offices of Julie A. Marquis, (415) 385-0755, appearing for Defts., William Aarsheim, Britton Womack, and Q. Surgical LLC, & opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Plft's Motion to Stay Dissolution is GRANTED, Plft. IS ENTITLED TO THE REQUESTED RELIEF UNDER CORPORATIONS CODE SECTION 2000(b).Plft's OSC re Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANT SPARKS. COURT ISSUES AN INJUNCTION AGAINST DEF. SPARKS THAT MIRRORS THE RESTRAINING ORDER ENTERED ON 2/23/09. SPARKS IS ALSO ORDERED TO RETURN ALL OF SOURCE SURGICAL'S PROPERTY, INCLUDING THE COMPUTER BACK UP TAPES (To be returned within a week of the Prel. Order.) PLAINTIFF TO POST A $250,000 BOND PER CCP 529. DENIED AS TO DEFTS AARSHEIM AND WOMACK. PLAINTIFF IS UNLIKELY TO PREVAIL AGAINT THEM ON ITS MISAPPROPRIATION CLAIM BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH THE EXISTENCE OF A TRADE SECRET. See American Paper & Packaging Products, Inc. v. Kirgan, (1986) 183 Cal.App.3d 1318, 1326. PLAINTIFF IS UNLIKELY TO PREVAIL ON ITS BREACH OF TRADE SECRETS CONTRACT CLAIM BECAUSE IT IS UNCLEAR WHETHER WOMACK EXECUTED THAT AGREEMENT. MOREOVER, PLFT'S REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AGAINST AARSHEIM AND WOMACK VIOLATES BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 16600 AS AN ILLEGAL RESTRAINT OF TRADE. The Court adds: Injunction is denied as to Defendant Q. Counsel to submit list of Doc. filed, for the Crt. The Court will prepare Order re: Evid. Object. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.(302/CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5759
